Section." There put a brief 속l... So, I'm ready for you. Alright, what's up? Good, very good. How's things? Cool, I'm after a few. You're on a 20? I think the shorts are funny. I'm like upset because I know what I'm supposed to be on the scale. Like, Davey, I'm on the scale, I'm on the standing count. I'm supposed to count it then, you know. So, what is special? What happens if you lose a pound a day for the next year? You get weight negative 200. You can't lose a pound a day every day. You gotta understand a lot of that is water. And when people first begin this type of program, they go from eating just whatever to eating fairly low-cloaked diet and clean. Low sodium, less processed food. So what happens is your water retention starts to drop dramatically. People usually hold a lot of water, a lot of inflammation, a lot of bloating. And when they start something like this right out of the bath, a lot of detox is cleansed out and loses a lot of excess water. So it's not like you're losing a lot of fat in that first week, you're losing a lot of water. And eventually, once that stabilizes, there's only so much you're gonna drop on that. Now, it's gonna taper off and slow down, but when you start losing weight now, it's gonna be actually fat. So water comes off like a gain. So literally like they want you to lose like three pounds of water just because you're madly eating the tapered chips and cookies. So don't even get caught up on that. You gotta understand something. Prior to beginning anything like this, right, people are generally gaining weight every month or every year. Because no one is born here. You're small, you grow, you become an adult and then you're really young. But when you're 16, 17, you probably weigh less than you do today. And over time, bad habits get in control. At some point, and at this point usually it's when they start, they make a U-turn. And they start going down. Now once you do that, it doesn't matter how fast that happens, because you're so young that all of you are having this role right now is time. It's not like you're 70 and you gotta hit a certain weight when you're 73. It doesn't matter. You're 20 years old. As long as you made that U-turn and you're conscious of what you're doing, and you haven't found doing it, success is guaranteed. It's just a matter of how long you're gonna stay. So those meal plans are designed to give you what you need but on a minimal basis. They're relatively deprived of energy or calories. They're fairly low. They have a pretty decent calorie deficit. When you're skipping them, you really, really put yourself in a deep hole. Chlorically. And that is going to be detrimental to your slow down progress. Under-eating is just as bad and often worse, especially for men than overeating. Because men rely on our muscle in order to keep the fat off. We have naturally more muscle than women do. And when we lose the ability to hold on to that muscle, the amount of fat they're able to burn or the basement of our crate drops. And your ability to lose and keep fat off with less muscle is going to be really bad. Your energy is going to be really bad. You have to support the muscle. And that means you've got to get that protein. You've got to get it. And she's got to learn to keep it. Well, now it's finally that I have a protein that I'm going to first and then I'll be the next.
